lede: A look at August 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Tidjane Salaün and Ryan Eggold, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Israel Defense Forces
events.desc: Israel strikes Al-Tabaeen school in eastern Gaza City, killing at least 80 Palestinians.

events.title: Typhoon Lekima
events.desc: Thirty-two are killed and one million are evacuated as Typhoon Lekima makes landfall in Zhejiang, China. Earlier it had caused flooding in the Philippines.

events.title: Murder of Adam Walsh
events.desc: Murder of Adam Walsh: The head of John Walsh's son is found. This inspires the creation of the television series America's Most Wanted and the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children.

events.title: Heron Road Workers Memorial Bridge
events.desc: The Heron Road Bridge collapses while being built, killing nine workers in the deadliest construction accident in both Ottawa and Ontario.

events.title: First Indochina War
events.desc: First Indochina War: The French Union withdraws its forces from Operation Camargue against the Viet Minh in central Vietnam.

events.title: National Party (Uruguay)
events.desc: After Uruguay's governing Blanco Party refuses Brazil's demands, José Antônio Saraiva announces that the Brazilian military will begin reprisals, beginning the Uruguayan War.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Wilson's Creek: A mixed force of Confederate, Missouri State Guard, and Arkansas State troops defeat outnumbered attacking Union forces in the southwestern part of the state.

events.title: French Revolution
events.desc: French Revolution: Storming of the Tuileries Palace: Louis XVI is arrested and taken into custody as his Swiss Guards are massacred by the Parisian mob.

events.title: Vasa (ship)
events.desc: The Swedish warship Vasa sinks on her maiden voyage off Stockholm.

events.title: Yekuno Amlak
events.desc: Yekuno Amlak takes the imperial throne of Ethiopia, restoring the Solomonic dynasty to power after a 100-year Zagwe interregnum.

births.name: William Willett
births.line: English inventor, founded British Summer Time (died 1915)

deaths.text: Jeffrey Epstein, American financier and child sex trafficker

observances.text: Christian feast day: Nuestra Señora del Buen Suceso de Parañaque, Patroness of Parañaque, Philippines
